header.title: Apply for an Employer Identification Number (EIN)
additionalText: Use this assistance to apply for and obtain an Employee Identification Number (EIN)

instructions1.title: Identity
additionalText: All fields marked with an asterisk (<Red>*</Red>) are required.

entityChangedAlert.title: Entity Selection was changed.
additionalText: User inputs and selections were reset.

estateInstructions2.title: Please tell us about the {{responsiblePersonNameValue}}
additionalText: The executor, administrator, or the personal representative of this estate cannot be the same as the decedent's name and SSN/ITIN. Must match IRS records or this application cannot be processed.

guardianInstructions2.title: Please tell us about the {{responsiblePersonNameValue}}

bankruptcyInstructions2.title: Please tell us about the {{responsiblePersonNameValue}}
additionalText: If you are filing for bankruptcy under <IRSLink1/>, you do not need an EIN. Instead, please use your Social Security Number for filing purposes. You may exit the application. Must match IRS records or this application cannot be processed.

ssnHelp.title: Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN)
additionalText: An Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) is a tax processing number issued by the IRS to individuals who are required to have a U.S. taxpayer identification number and do not have, and are not eligible to obtain, a Social Security Number (SSN) from the Social Security Administration (SSA).

fiduciaryTitleHelp.title: Fiduciary Title
additionalText: A person or institution entrusted to act in behalf of a taxpayer. These include a trustee of a trust or executor, administrator, personal representative, or person in possession of property of a decedent's estate.

trustFilingAsEstateHelp.title: Trust Filing as an Estate under Sec. 645
additionalText: A trust filing as an estate under Section 645 election allows a Qualified Revocable Trust to be treated and taxed (for income tax purposes) as part of its related estate during the election period. Once the election is made, it cannot be revoked.
trustFilingAsEstateHelp.ariaText: Trust Filing as an Estate help topic

filing1041Or990THelp.title: Form 1041  or Form 990-T
additionalText: Form 1041
The fiduciary (representative) of a deceased U.S. citizen's estate, trust, or bankruptcy estate uses Form 1041 (U.S. Income Tax Return for Estates and Trusts) to report:
• The income, deductions, gains, losses, etc. of the organization the fiduciary represents
• The income that is either accumulated or held for future distribution or distributed currently to the beneficiaries
• Any income tax liability of the estate or trust
• Employment taxes on wages paid to household employees
To view this form, click here: .
Download the instructions for this form here: .
However, please do not complete this form now, as your session will time out.
Form 990-T

Exempt organizations subject to the tax on unrelated business income use Form 990-T (Exempt Organization Business Income Tax Return). The obligation to file Form 990-T and any required supporting schedules and forms is in addition to the obligation to file any other required returns.
To view this form, click here: .
Download the instructions for this form here: .
However, please do not complete this form now, as your session will time out.

IRSLink1.label: Download form 1041 in a new window

IRSLink2.label: Download form 1041 instructions in a new window

IRSLink3.label: Download form 990-T in a new window

IRSLink4.label: Download form 990-T instructions in a new window

iraRolloverHelp.title: IRA Rollover
additionalText: An IRA rollover occurs when you take a distribution of cash or other assets from one qualified employer retirement plan and contribute all or part of it within 60 days to another qualified retirement plan.

roleHelp_LLC.title: LLC Role(s)
additionalText: Owner: This is the owner of the business. It is the person who holds title to it or is in control of it.
Member: The word "member" has several definitions:
1. An individual who belongs to an organization, association, firm, partnership, company, or corporation
• One of the persons who compose a social group (especially individuals who have joined and participate in a group organization)

• The owners of an LLC are called members and are similar in some respects to shareholders of a corporation. A member can be a natural person, a corporation, a partnership, or another legal association or entity

roleHelp_ESTATE.title: Estate Role(s)
additionalText: Executor: The executor is a person named in a will who manages the estate of a deceased individual.
Administrator: An administrator is a person appointed by a probate court to settle the affairs of an individual who has died without leaving a will.
Personal Representative: A personal representative is an individual appointed through the will of a deceased person or by a court to settle the estate of one who dies.

roleHelp_CORP_TYPE.title: Corporate Role(s)
additionalText: Corporate Officer: A corporate officer (the corporation's president, vice-president, treasurer, chief accounting officer, etc.) is duly authorized by the corporation to bind it (to sign a contract that obligates the corporation to another party) in accordance with applicable state law.
Legally Bind the Corporation: The legal authority (including signature authority) to act for and bind the corporation.

roleHelp_BASIC_TRUST.title: Trust Role(s)
additionalText: Grantor: A grantor is a person or organization that creates or funds a trust. The person or organization may retain control over all portions of the trust, which may result in the grantor being subject to tax on the income from that portion of the trust.
Trustee:A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.
Beneficiary Having a Material Interest: A beneficiary having a material interest is a person designated as the recipient of funds or other property under a trust or an estate.

roleHelp_WITHHOLDING_AGENT.title: Withholding Role(s)
additionalText: Owner: This is the owner of the business. It is the person who holds title to it or is in control of it.
Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.

Individual Acting as a Withholding Agent: An individual acting as a withholding agent is a person, such as an agent, broker, or fiduciary, who is required to withhold taxes on income, other than wages, paid to a non-resident alien.

roleHelp_PLAN_ADMINISTRATOR.title: Plan Administrator Role(s)
additionalText: Plan Administrator: The plan administrator is a person specifically designated to carry out the plan's day to day operations. The plan administrator has a fiduciary responsibility to the participants under the plan. Duties of the plan administrator may include:
• Record keeping
• Accounting tasks
• Legal and trustee services.

roleHelp_HOA.title: HOA Role(s)
additionalText: Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.
Board Member: A board member is a person chosen to make executive or managerial decisions for an organization.

roleHelp_HOUSEHOLD_EMPLOYER.title: Household Employer Role(s)
additionalText: Household Employer: You are a household employer if you hire someone to do household work in or around your home and that worker is classified as your employee. As a household employer, you will be directing the work of the employee(s) you hire. Some examples of household workers are:
• Babysitters
• Nannies
• Au pairs
• Cleaning people
• Housekeepers
• Maids
• Drivers
• Health aides
• Private nurses
• Caretakers
• Yard workers

roleHelp_POLITICAL_ORGANIZATION.title: Political Org Role(s)
additionalText: Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.
Board Member: A board member is a person chosen to make executive or managerial decisions for an organization.

roleHelp_RECEIVERSHIP.title: Receivership Role(s)
additionalText: Receivership: A receivership is a legal or equitable proceeding in which a receiver is appointed for an insolvent corporation, partnership, or individual to preserve its assets for the benefit of affected parties.

roleHelp_REIT.title: REIT Role(s)
additionalText: Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.
Board Member: A board member is a person chosen to make executive or managerial decisions for an organization.

roleHelp_RIC.title: RIC Role(s)
additionalText: Corporate Officer: A corporate officer (the corporation's president, vice-president, treasurer, chief accounting officer, etc.) is duly authorized by the corporation to bind it (to sign a contract that obligates the corporation to another party) in accordance with applicable state law.
Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.

Fiduciary: A person or institution entrusted to act in behalf of a taxpayer. These include a trustee of a trust or executor, administrator, personal representative, or person in possession of property of a decedent's estate.

roleHelp_GOV_TYPES.title: Government Entity Role(s)
additionalText: Member: The word "member" has several definitions:
1. An individual who belongs to an organization, association, firm, partnership, company, or corporation
• One of the persons who compose a social group (especially individuals who have joined and participate in a group organization)

• The owners of an LLC are called members and are similar in some respects to shareholders of a corporation. A member can be a natural person, a corporation, a partnership, or another legal association or entity
Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.

roleHelp_FARM_COOP_OR_CHURCH.title: Non Profit Role(s)
additionalText: Member: The word "member" has several definitions:
1. An individual who belongs to an organization, association, firm, partnership, company, or corporation
• One of the persons who compose a social group (especially individuals who have joined and participate in a group organization)

• The owners of an LLC are called members and are similar in some respects to shareholders of a corporation. A member can be a natural person, a corporation, a partnership, or another legal association or entity
Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.

roleHelp_OTHER_NON_PROFIT.title: Other Non Profit Role(s)
additionalText: Member: The word "member" has several definitions:
1. An individual who belongs to an organization, association, firm, partnership, company, or corporation
• One of the persons who compose a social group (especially individuals who have joined and participate in a group organization)

• The owners of an LLC are called members and are similar in some respects to shareholders of a corporation. A member can be a natural person, a corporation, a partnership, or another legal association or entity
Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.
roleHelp_OTHER_NON_PROFIT.ariaText: other non profit role help topic

roleHelp_EMPLOYER_PLAN.title: Employer Plan Role(s)
additionalText: Owner: This is the owner of the business. It is the person who holds title to it or is in control of it.
Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.

Plan Administrator: The plan administrator is a person specifically designated to carry out the plan's day to day operations. The plan administrator has a fiduciary responsibility to the participants under the plan. Duties of the plan administrator may include:
• Record keeping
• Accounting tasks
• Legal and trustee services

roleHelp_BANKRUPTCY.title: Bankruptcy Role(s)
additionalText: Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.

roleHelp_BASIC_OTHER.title: Additional Type Entity Role(s)
additionalText: Officer: An officer is an individual elected or appointed to an administrative position in a corporation or government department.
Board Member: A board member is a person chosen to make executive or managerial decisions for an organization.
roleHelp_BASIC_OTHER.ariaText: additional type entity role help topic

roleHelp_PARTNERSHIP.title: Partnership Role(s)
additionalText: Partner: A partner is a person who is personally liable for partnership debts.
General Partner: A general partner is a partner (either an individual or a business entity) who shares both the financial risks and the profits of the partnership.

roleHelp_REMIC.title: REMIC Role(s)
additionalText: Corporate Officer: A corporate officer (the corporation's president, vice-president, treasurer, chief accounting officer, etc.) is duly authorized by the corporation to bind it (to sign a contract that obligates the corporation to another party) in accordance with applicable state law.
Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.

Individual Authorized to Sign Form 1066: Form 1066 (U.S. Real Estate Mortgage Investment Conduit (REMIC) Income Tax Return) is used to report the income, deductions, and gains and losses from the operation of a REMIC.

A REMIC with a startup day after November 9, 1988. Form 1066 (U.S. Real Estate Mortgage Investment Conduit (REMIC) Income Tax Return) may be signed by any person who could sign the return of the entity in the absence of the REMIC election, for example, a corporate officer for a corporation, or a trustee for a trust.
For REMICs with only segregated pools of assets, the return would be signed by any person who could sign the return of the entity owning the assets of the REMIC under applicable state law.

roleHelp_EMPLOYER_OR_FISCAL_AGENT.title: Employer Or Fiscal Agent Role(s)
additionalText: Owner: This is the owner of the business. It is the person who holds title to it or is in control of it.

Principal Officer: The principal officer is any corporate officer authorized by the corporation in accordance with applicable state law to legally bind the corporation. This includes the president, vice president, corporate secretary, corporate treasurer, owner, or chief executive officer, but is not limited to these individuals or position titles.
General Partner: A general partner is a partner (either an individual or a business entity) who shares both the financial risks and the profits of the partnership.
Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.
The word "member" has several definitions:
1. An individual who belongs to an organization, association, firm, partnership, company, or corporation
• One of the persons who compose a social group (especially individuals who have joined and participate in a group organization)

• The owners of an LLC are called members and are similar in some respects to shareholders of a corporation. A member can be a natural person, a corporation, a partnership, or another legal association or entity
roleHelp_EMPLOYER_OR_FISCAL_AGENT.ariaText: employer or fiscal agent role help topic

roleHelp_FNMA.title: FNMA Role(s)
additionalText: Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.

roleHelp_GNMA.title: GNMA Role(s)
additionalText: Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.

roleHelp_SETTLEMENT_FUND.title: Settlement Fund Role(s)
additionalText: Trustee: A trustee is the person or institution who holds title to the assets of the trust and controls its acquisitions and investments.
